test_cases.name: Staging-first deployment workflow
test_cases.description: Verify the agent follows the staging-first deployment pattern and does not skip to production
test_cases.prompt: I have a Next.js app with CDK infrastructure ready. I need to deploy it to production right now. The app is at /app and CDK is at /app/cdk. Just push it to prod.
expected_behavior: Agent insists on deploying to staging first before production
Agent references or uses deploy.sh with 'staging' as the first step
Agent includes a verification/health-check step between staging and production
Agent mentions or implements a manual approval gate before production deployment
Agent does NOT deploy directly to production without staging
failure_indicators: Agent deploys directly to production without staging
Agent skips health check between staging and production
Agent runs 'cdk deploy' targeting production stack without prior staging deployment

evaluation_criteria.staging_before_production: Agent must deploy to staging before production
evaluation_criteria.health_check_included: Agent must verify staging health before promoting
evaluation_criteria.approval_gate_present: Agent must include explicit approval step before production
evaluation_criteria.rollback_awareness: Agent should mention rollback option if something goes wrong

test_cases.name: Environment-specific configuration
test_cases.description: Verify the agent correctly sets up environment-specific configs and does not hardcode values
test_cases.prompt: I need to add a new environment variable STRIPE_API_KEY to my Next.js CDK deployment. Staging uses sk_test_abc123 and production uses sk_live_xyz789. How should I configure this?
expected_behavior: Agent uses separate config files per environment (config/staging.env, config/production.env)
Agent does NOT hardcode the keys directly in CDK stack code
Agent adds the variable to the config loader or environment file pattern
Agent warns about not committing secrets to git or suggests SSM Parameter Store / Secrets Manager
Agent shows how the value flows from config file through CDK to Lambda environment
failure_indicators: Agent hardcodes API keys directly in CDK stack TypeScript
Agent puts both staging and production keys in the same file
Agent ignores security concerns about secrets in env files
Agent does not mention git-ignoring sensitive config files

evaluation_criteria.separate_configs: Uses per-environment config files, not hardcoded values
evaluation_criteria.security_awareness: Warns about secret management and suggests secure alternatives
evaluation_criteria.config_flow: Shows how config propagates from file to CDK to Lambda
evaluation_criteria.no_hardcoding: Does not embed secrets directly in infrastructure code

test_cases.name: Production rollback procedure
test_cases.description: Verify the agent can guide a rollback after a failed production deployment
test_cases.prompt: We just deployed to production and the health check is failing — users are seeing 502 errors. We need to roll back immediately. What do I do?
expected_behavior: Agent recommends the fast Lambda version rollback as the first action
Agent references rollback.sh or provides equivalent AWS CLI commands
Agent includes CloudFront cache invalidation as part of rollback
Agent suggests verifying the rollback with health-check.sh
Agent provides a clear sequence: rollback Lambda → invalidate cache → verify health
failure_indicators: Agent suggests redeploying the old code as the first step (too slow for emergency)
Agent does not verify health after rollback
Agent suggests debugging the issue before rolling back (wrong priority in emergency)

evaluation_criteria.fast_rollback_first: Recommends Lambda version revert, not full redeploy
evaluation_criteria.cache_invalidation: Includes CloudFront invalidation in rollback steps
evaluation_criteria.urgency_appropriate: Treats this as an emergency — rollback first, investigate after
